L1.area: Middleware for caching and securing authentication tokens

L2.description: The middleware uses weak cryptographic primitives for protecting cached authentication data. It employs an inadequate hashing algorithm for computing message authentication codes, uses a simplistic key derivation scheme instead of a proper key derivation function, and does not perform constant-time comparison when verifying signatures, making it vulnerable to timing attacks and data forgery.

L3.area: Middleware for caching and securing authentication tokens
L3.description: The middleware uses weak cryptographic primitives for protecting cached authentication data. It employs an inadequate hashing algorithm for computing message authentication codes, uses a simplistic key derivation scheme instead of a proper key derivation function, and does not perform constant-time comparison when verifying signatures, making it vulnerable to timing attacks and data forgery.

ground_truth.reason: The memcache encryption middleware uses weak cryptographic primitives including SHA1 for MAC computation, a simple SHA256 hash for key derivation instead of a proper KDF, and non-constant-time comparison for signature verification, allowing attackers to bypass cache data protection.
